title: Close Unused Tabs
instruction: Close or bookmark tabs until only the tab for the task you need right now is left open.
why_it_matters: A smaller visual field reduces switching friction.

title: Write Three Open Loops
instruction: Write down the three unresolved things taking up the most mental space.
why_it_matters: Named loose ends are easier to handle than vague background noise.

title: Set a Single-Task Block
instruction: Put a 10-minute timer on one task and keep only that task visible until it ends.
why_it_matters: A short protected block lowers restart time.

title: Clear Your Desktop Surface
instruction: Remove everything from your desk except the items you need for the next block of work.
why_it_matters: A cleaner physical field reduces visual pull.

title: List Tomorrow's First Step
instruction: Write tomorrow's first action on a note where you will see it first.
why_it_matters: A visible first step removes morning decision time.

title: Mute Notifications
instruction: Turn off phone and desktop notifications for the next 15 minutes.
why_it_matters: Removing interruptions gives attention time to settle.

title: Sort Your Notes Inbox
instruction: Move unfiled notes into named folders or delete them until your notes inbox is empty.
why_it_matters: A cleaner note pile makes useful information easier to find.

title: Delete Low-Value Reminders
instruction: Delete or dismiss five reminders, alarms, or notifications you already know you will not use.
why_it_matters: Fewer false prompts make important prompts easier to notice.

title: Rewrite One Task
instruction: Rewrite one vague task as a single physical action you can start.
why_it_matters: Specific language lowers the cost of starting.

title: Review Yesterday's Notes
instruction: Read yesterday's notes and mark one item that still matters today.
why_it_matters: A quick review keeps the signal and drops the rest.

title: Choose the Smallest Next Step
instruction: Write the next physical step you need to do, then do only that step for five minutes.
why_it_matters: A small target is easier to enter than a broad task.

title: File Loose Papers
instruction: File or discard five loose papers that are still sitting out.
why_it_matters: Loose paper stacks create small repeated decisions.

title: Rename Recent Downloads
instruction: Rename and move the ten most recent files in your downloads folder.
why_it_matters: Named files are faster to reuse than mystery files.

title: Empty One Bag
instruction: Empty one backpack, tote, or purse and put everything back in a clear place.
why_it_matters: Hidden clutter often creates repeated searching later.

title: Choose Homes for Loose Items
instruction: Pick homes for three items that keep ending up in random places.
why_it_matters: Repeat items stop creating repeat mess when they have a stable home.

title: Archive Old Calendar Holds
instruction: Delete or update calendar holds that are already outdated.
why_it_matters: An accurate calendar is easier to trust.

title: Set Out Tomorrow's Essentials
instruction: Place tomorrow's key items in one visible spot before you stop for the day.
why_it_matters: Preparation reduces next-day scramble time.

title: Throw Away Old Receipts
instruction: Throw away or file receipts that no longer need action.
why_it_matters: Expired paperwork keeps attention tied up for no return.

title: Create One Catch-All Tray
instruction: Set up one tray or box for items that need sorting later and move current loose items into it.
why_it_matters: A controlled landing zone prevents clutter from spreading.

title: Repack Your Work Bag
instruction: Remove everything from your work bag and pack back only what you need this week.
why_it_matters: A lighter bag makes daily setup faster.

title: Label One Storage Spot
instruction: Add a simple label to one drawer, shelf, or bin you use often.
why_it_matters: Clear labels reduce future searching and re-deciding.

title: Put Away Ten Items
instruction: Put away ten visible items in the room you are already in.
why_it_matters: A short reset lowers visual clutter quickly.

title: Fill a Water Bottle
instruction: Fill the bottle or glass you are most likely to drink from for the rest of today.
why_it_matters: Hydration is easier when the container is already in reach.

title: Walk for Ten Minutes
instruction: Take a brisk 10-minute walk indoors or outside without stopping to check your phone.
why_it_matters: A short walk increases movement without a long setup.

title: Prep One Protein Snack
instruction: Prepare one simple snack with protein and put it where you can grab it later.
why_it_matters: Prepared food is easier to use than good intentions.

title: Do One Mobility Circuit
instruction: Do one round of neck, shoulder, hip, and ankle mobility work at an easy pace.
why_it_matters: A short circuit breaks up physical stiffness from sitting.

title: Set Out Workout Clothes
instruction: Put tomorrow's workout or walking clothes in one ready-to-wear stack.
why_it_matters: Prepared clothes remove one early friction point.

title: Make Tomorrow's Lunch Base
instruction: Prepare the main part of tomorrow's lunch so only final assembly is left.
why_it_matters: Partially prepared meals are easier to finish and use.

title: Stand Outside for Five Minutes
instruction: Step outside for five minutes and stay standing or walking the whole time.
why_it_matters: A brief change of environment can interrupt a low-energy loop.

title: Replace Desk Snack Clutter
instruction: Clear food clutter from your desk and leave one easier option or water in reach.
why_it_matters: The default option usually becomes the used option.

title: Do Twenty Bodyweight Reps
instruction: Do 20 total slow squats, wall pushups, or sit-to-stands with full control.
why_it_matters: A small strength block is easier to fit than a full workout.

title: Prep Breakfast Base
instruction: Set out or prepare the base of tomorrow's breakfast before you leave the kitchen.
why_it_matters: A prepared start makes the morning easier to handle.

title: Take a Five-Minute Walk
instruction: Walk for five minutes indoors or outside at a steady pace.
why_it_matters: A short walk is a low-friction way to change state.

title: Send One Blocking Reply
instruction: Reply to the message that is currently holding up the most progress.
why_it_matters: One unblock can move more work than one more draft.

title: Update Your Top Task List
instruction: Cut your current work list down to the three tasks that still matter most.
why_it_matters: A shorter list is easier to act on than a full inventory.

title: Close One Small Loop
instruction: Finish one small work task that has been sitting half-done.
why_it_matters: Closed loops reduce drag on larger work.

title: Draft Tomorrow's First Block
instruction: Write a short plan for your first focused work block tomorrow.
why_it_matters: A defined first block lowers startup time.

title: Clean Meeting Notes
instruction: Turn the last meeting's notes into a short list of actions, owners, and dates.
why_it_matters: Clean notes reduce repeated re-reading.

title: Archive Old Threads
instruction: Archive email or chat threads that no longer need your attention.
why_it_matters: A cleaner inbox makes active work easier to see.

title: Review One Key Number
instruction: Check one business number you track and note what changed from the last period.
why_it_matters: A small review keeps important metrics from drifting out of view.

title: Prepare One Meeting Agenda
instruction: Write a short agenda for one upcoming meeting and send it before the meeting starts.
why_it_matters: A clear agenda reduces meeting drift.

title: Delegate or Delete a Stalled Task
instruction: Choose one stalled task and either assign it, rescope it, or remove it from your list.
why_it_matters: Stalled tasks consume attention even when no work is happening.

title: File One Loose Document
instruction: Rename and file one work document that is still hard to find.
why_it_matters: Findable files reduce repeat searching and duplicate work.

title: Finish the Smallest Overdue Task
instruction: Pick the smallest overdue work task and finish it now.
why_it_matters: A quick finish reduces backlog drag immediately.

title: Open the Project and Name the Next Step
instruction: Open one active project and write the next visible step before doing anything else.
why_it_matters: Named next steps make project re-entry faster.

title: Collect the Materials
instruction: Put the files, tools, or notes for one project in one place.
why_it_matters: Setup work is easier when materials are already gathered.

title: Work on One Visible Piece
instruction: Spend 15 minutes finishing one visible piece of the project instead of planning the whole thing.
why_it_matters: Visible progress keeps projects from staying abstract.

title: Write a Progress Note
instruction: Write five lines on what is done, what is blocked, and what comes next.
why_it_matters: A short record reduces restart friction later.

title: Fix One Small Defect
instruction: Fix one obvious defect, error, or rough edge in an active project.
why_it_matters: Small repairs keep problems from piling up.

title: Sort the Backlog
instruction: Split your project backlog into two lists named next and later.
why_it_matters: Fewer active options make projects easier to move.

title: Delete Unused Drafts
instruction: Delete unused drafts, duplicate files, or old versions from one active project.
why_it_matters: Less clutter makes the current version easier to work on.

title: Sketch a Rough Version
instruction: Make a rough version of one part of the project without trying to polish it.
why_it_matters: Rough output moves a project forward faster than more delay.

title: Set This Week's Finish Line
instruction: Define one specific thing this project should have by the end of the week.
why_it_matters: A near-term finish line narrows project scope.

title: Gather Three References
instruction: Collect three references, examples, or source materials related to one current project.
why_it_matters: Useful inputs reduce wandering when work starts.

title: Make One Visible Change
instruction: Open the project, make one visible change, and stop after five minutes.
why_it_matters: Small visible movement keeps a project active.

title: Send One Thank-You Text
instruction: Send one short thank-you text for something specific someone did recently.
why_it_matters: Specific appreciation is easier to receive and remember.

title: Reply to One Delayed Message
instruction: Reply to one message you have been leaving unanswered.
why_it_matters: One delayed reply often carries more drag than it should.

title: Put One Call on the Calendar
instruction: Schedule one call, meal, or walk with a person you want to stay in touch with.
why_it_matters: Specific plans are more likely to happen than loose intentions.

title: Ask One Specific Question
instruction: Send one person a direct question that is easy for them to answer.
why_it_matters: Specific questions are easier to respond to than vague check-ins.

title: Leave a Short Voice Note
instruction: Send one short voice note instead of another typed message.
why_it_matters: Voice can carry tone more clearly than text.

title: Write a Two-Sentence Check-In
instruction: Send one plain two-sentence check-in with one fact about you and one question for them.
why_it_matters: A simple structure makes check-ins easier to send.

title: Remove One Loose Obligation
instruction: Close out one social obligation you will not do instead of leaving it vague.
why_it_matters: Clear communication prevents low-grade social drag.

title: Plan One Shared Meal or Walk
instruction: Propose one specific meal or walk with a day, time, and place.
why_it_matters: Specific proposals are easier to accept or adjust.

title: Review Contacts and Pick One Person
instruction: Scan your contacts and choose one person to reconnect with this week.
why_it_matters: A quick review surfaces people who otherwise stay out of sight.

title: Confirm One Loose Plan
instruction: Confirm one half-made plan instead of letting it stay uncertain.
why_it_matters: Confirmed plans reduce follow-up drift.

title: Send a Plain Check-In
instruction: Send one plain check-in message to a person you care about.
why_it_matters: A short check-in keeps contact from slipping further.
